title: Biathle and triathle leagues heat things up ahead of Eden Championships
description: SPORT NEWS - The Eden MPA hosted another...
bodyText:
Gallery

SPORT NEWS - The Eden MPA hosted another thrilling round of its biathle and triathle leagues on Saturday 10 May.


> With only two league events remaining before the Eden Championships, the race to qualify for the 2025 UIPM World Championships is heating up.


Athletes from various districts gathered in preparation for the run-up to the most prestigious event of the season: the UIPM Biathle, Triathle and Laser Run World Championships, set to take place in Mossel Bay in December 2025.

With the global spotlight firmly on the Garden Route, this championship is expected to be one of the largest international sporting events the region has ever hosted.

Under mounting pressure, performances reached new heights - particularly in the junior men and u.19 categories.

PHOTO GALLERY: The Eden MPA biathle and triathle leagues

These athletes delivered blistering times, demonstrating exceptional endurance, speed and precision across the swim, shoot and run segments. Their efforts weren’t just impressive, they were world-class.


> Top performers of the day included Franna Vorster, Andreas Rossouw and Dirk Rossouw (senior category); Josua Vorster and Marco Erasmus (junior category); Johan Schoeman and Dune Haasbroek (u.19); Annabelle McEwan (u.17); Ruan Share (u.15); Keyla Douglas and Wanru Haasbroek (u.13); Joshua Burrow (u.11); and Lukas van Wyngaard (u.9).


In the masters category, Gavin Gericke, Louis Botha, Anita Louw (60+) and Seanette Botha (50+) also delivered outstanding performances.

Daniel van Wyngaard, Keyla Douglas, Riley van Wyk, Wanru Haasbroek, Anita Louw and Ruan Share.

Marco Erasmus and Dune Haasbroek.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
